_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Epididymal tuberculosis resulting in caseation and a firm expanding mass not responding to antibiotic treatment

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with history and examination and planned line of treatment

ppd_preauth.q: Were the patient's clinical history/investigations indicative of the Procedure?
Whether the investigation reports confirm the diagnosis?

common_queries: Patient's clinical history or investigations are not indicative of Epididymal Excision.
Investigation reports do not confirm the diagnosis for Epididymal Excision.
Clinical notes are incomplete (missing history, examination, planned line of treatment, or indications for the procedure).
USG Scrotum report is missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary is incomplete (missing follow-up advice).

clinical_key_pointers: Epididymal excision or Epididymectomy is an operation that is seldom indicated and is usually used as a last resort in treating pain.

The main indications include post-vasectomy epididymal engorgement, complex epididymal cyst disease, chronic recurrent epididymitis, and epididymal tuberculosis resulting in caseation and a firm expanding mass not responding to antibiotic treatment.
